channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Name That Toon Contest
item.description: No summary available
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Coupang hit with record $409 million data breach fine in Korea
item.description: ​​The Personal Information Protection Commission (PIPC), South Korea's data protection regulator, has fined e-commerce giant Coupang a record 624.6 billion won (roughly $409 million) following a massi...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: CISA tells govt agencies to patch critical exploited flaws in 3 days
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) announced a new Binding Operational Directive, 26-04, that prioritizes security updates for Federal Civilian Executive Branch (FCEB) ag...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: AI Broke Vulnerability Management. That's Why CISOs Are Moving Budget to BAS.
item.description: For thirty years, vulnerability management ran on a buffer: the months between when a vulnerability was found and when someone could figure out how to weaponize it. The solution was straightforward en...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: OceanLotus Hits Vietnam Investors With SPECTRALVIPER in FireAnt Attack
item.description:
The Vietnam-aligned threat actor known as OceanLotus has been attributed to two distinct campaigns that targeted domestic entities and stock investors with a backdoor known as SPECTRALVIPER.
The camp...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft fixes BitLocker recovery bug on Windows Server 2025
item.description: Microsoft has resolved a known issue causing some Windows Server 2025 devices to boot into BitLocker recovery after installing the April 2026 security update. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Nottingham University data breach affects over 450,000 students
item.description: The University of Nottingham confirmed on Wednesday that a hacking group gained access to its student records system in a breach affecting both current students and alums. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: GitHub to Disable npm Install Scripts by Default to Stop Supply Chain Attacks
item.description:
GitHub has announced what it said are "breaking changes" coming to npm version 12, one of which turns off install scripts by default to combat software supply chain threats.
The changes aim to combat...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Max severity Ivanti Sentry vulnerability now exploited in attacks
item.description: Attackers are now targeting a recently patched maximum-severity flaw in Ivanti Sentry, enabling them to execute code with root privileges on Internet-exposed secure mobile gateways. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Chinese, N. Korean Threat Groups Build on Asia-Pacific Success
item.description: North Korea's gross domestic product (GDP) has grown, in part because of the cybercrime gains of groups linked to the nation, which target business and financial firms....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Path traversal flaw in AI dev platform Langflow exploited in attacks
item.description: Attackers are actively exploiting CVE-2026-5027, a high-severity path traversal vulnerability in the AI development platform Langflow, to write arbitrary files on exposed servers. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: CISA Rewrites Federal Patching Requirements for AI Threat Era
item.description: The new directive gives federal agencies three days to fix the most dangerous flaws, while less severe issues can be deferred....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: The ‘Miasma’ worm source code briefly leaked on GitHub
item.description: The Miasma credential-stealing attack framework, which has recently targeted open-source ecosystems through supply-chain attacks, was briefly open-sourced on GitHub. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Bug Bounty Research Triggers ServiceNow Security Alert
item.description: Bug bounty research inadvertently led organizations to believe they were being breached through their ServiceNow instances....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: GitHub announces npm security changes to tackle supply-chain attacks
item.description: GitHub has announced that npm v12, expected next month, will introduce several security-focused changes aimed at blocking supply-chain attacks abusing behaviors triggered by the 'npm install' command....
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: AI Risk Worries Insurers and Businesses Alike
item.description: As companies adopt AI, many insurance firms are explicitly excluding AI risks, while others are forging ahead to create the right framework. What risks can firms reasonably manage?...
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Oracle PeopleSoft servers hacked in ShinyHunters data theft attacks
item.description: Oracle PeopleSoft servers are being targeted in ongoing data theft attacks by the ShinyHunters extortion gang, which claims to have stolen data from over 100 organizations. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Nightmare-Eclipse Drops Yet Another Microsoft Exploit, RoguePlanet
item.description: The disgruntled researcher released yet another PoC for a Windows Defender bug that allows for system takeover, showing no signs of abandoning their ongoing feud with Microsoft....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: China-Linked JDY Botnet Expands to 1,500+ Devices for Cyber Reconnaissance
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have warned of a "resurgence and expansion" of JDY, a covert network associated with China-nexus state-sponsored threat actors.
"The JDY botnet comprises over 1,500 SOHO [sm...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Ivanti, Fortinet, and SAP Release Patches for Multiple Critical Vulnerabilities
item.description:
Fortinet, Ivanti, and SAP have released security updates to address multiple critical security vulnerabilities that could result in arbitrary code execution and information disclosure.
The security f...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Unpatched Langflow Flaw CVE-2026-5027 Exploited for Unauthenticated RCE
item.description: A high-severity unpatched security flaw in Langflow, an open-source low-code platform to build artificial intelligence (AI) applications, has come under active exploitation in the wild, according to f...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: China-linked JDY botnet expands targeting of U.S. military networks
item.description: The JDY botnet, a malware network previously associated with Chinese threat actors like Volt Typhoon, has significantly expanded its targeting scope and reconnaissance efforts. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: CISA Adds Cisco, Chrome, and Arista Flaws to KEV Catalog Amid Active Exploitation
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday added three new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, following reports of active exploitati...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: The 5 Best Practices for Secure Identity Verification
item.description: Attackers are increasingly bypassing weak authentication through phishing, MFA fatigue, and service desk social engineering. Specops Software breaks down five best practices for stronger identity veri...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group ‘The Gentlemen?’
item.description: A cybercrime group known as The Gentlemen has emerged as the second most active ransomware gang by victim count, rapidly attracting a talented pool of hackers through an aggressive recruitment strateg...
item.dc:creator: Krebs on Security

item.title: Microsoft patches Exchange Server zero-day exploited in attacks
item.description: Microsoft has patched an actively exploited Exchange Server vulnerability that allows threat actors to execute arbitrary JavaScript code in cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks targeting Outlook Web Acc...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft: Some Windows PCs fail to install latest monthly updates
item.description: Microsoft warned customers on Tuesday that they may have issues installing the latest monthly updates on some Windows devices that were upgraded to Windows 11 24H2 or 25H2. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Your Automated Pentest Looks Clean. See What It Missed in This Expert Webinar
item.description:
Your pentest report looks clean. That might be the problem.
Run automated pentesting long enough, and the new findings start to dry up. By the third or fourth run, fewer issues appear. The report loo...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft patches YellowKey, GreenPlasma, MiniPlasma zero-days
item.description: On Tuesday, Microsoft patched two zero-day vulnerabilities that let attackers gain SYSTEM privileges on fully patched Windows systems, and a third one that grants access to BitLocker-protected drives....
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft Patches Record 206 Flaws, Including Three Zero-Days and Critical RCE Bugs
item.description:
Microsoft on Tuesday released fixes for a record 206 security vulnerabilities impacting its software portfolio, including three flaws that have been publicly disclosed at the time of release.
Of the ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News
